metrics.narrative: Business_Objectives__c and Value_to_Customer__c contain generic goals (faster time-to-ROI, AI-driven savings identification, unified S2P platform) logged during the qualification stage (circa Jan 2025), but no buyer-voiced, quantified figure (a dollar savings target, a cycle-time number) exists in any call, email, or task on record. The single Avoma call (28 Jan 2025, Adriel Picard + Zycus team) was purely social (time zones, a shared NASCAR event) and contained no business content.

sources: Task history (Jeffrey Buzila emails, procedural only)
champion.narrative: No champion exists. Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker role) and Jeffrey Buzila (RFP process coordinator) are the only two named contacts, and both have been purely procedural: Jeffrey manages RFP logistics and timeline updates, Adriel's only recorded interaction is a 2-minute social call on 28 Jan 2025. Neither has proactively advocated for Zycus, escalated internally, or driven the overdue presentation round back onto the calendar.

competition.narrative: Four named RFP competitors are on record: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba (Competitors__c field). SAP Ariba is explicitly the incumbent tool under evaluation for replacement ('Procurement Tool: Ariba (under evaluation for replacement)'), and the 8 Jul 2026 Next Step entry shows the deal team is still trying to confirm who inside SaskTel owns the SAP Ariba relationship decision. An additional, unnamed fifth vendor was mentioned only in an early qualification-era note (circa Jan 2025) and never carried into the formal RFP competitor field.

identify_pain.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c and How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_today__c (populated during qualification, circa Jan 2025) describe real, specific pain with the incumbent: 'high cost, low value' pricing, manual invoicing with no automation, a supplier portal vendors dislike, and a fragmented procurement stack. This is substantive pain, but it is roughly 18 months old and has never been re-confirmed verbally by Adriel Picard or Jeffrey Buzila on any call or email Zycus has on record since.

sources: Task history (no commercial content in any logged email)
paper_process.narrative: No commercial, pricing, MSA, or SOW conversation has occurred with SaskTel in any recorded call, email, or field. The deal is still at Shortlisted, one full stage short of Vendor Selected where paper process would normally begin.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false, and no C-level or budget-owning contact has ever appeared on this opportunity. Two CFOs and a EVP exist at the account level, but none is linked to this deal. As of the 8 Jul 2026 Next Step entry, the account owner is still trying to determine whether the CTO and the EVP even sit on the steering committee deciding the SAP Ariba replacement, meaning access to power remains unconfirmed 226 days into the Shortlisted stage.

decision_process.narrative: The process is unusually well-documented in dates: RFP issued 11 Feb 2026, Q&A deadline 18 Feb, buyer response to questions 25 Feb, submission deadline 11 Mar 2026 2:00pm CST, shortlist announcement the week of 6 Apr 2026, and a supplier presentation/interview round planned for the week of 20 Apr 2026. That presentation round is the one step that has not occurred as of 9 Jul 2026 (~11 weeks overdue), and no new date has been confirmed, so the process has stalled at its next-to-last known gate.

decision_criteria.narrative: A formal RFP with scored responses is confirmed: issued 11 Feb 2026, question deadlines through 25 Feb 2026, and a rep note dated 4 Jun 2026 records that the buyer's team is 'still reviewing responses to tally up scores.' However, Business_Requirements__c and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c are both empty, so the actual evaluation weighting or must-have criteria are not documented anywhere Zycus can see.

reasons.detail: SaskTel ($357,898, forecast Best Case, stage Shortlisted) has logged zero activity for 79 days. Mike Flowers' 2026-07-02 next-step note confirms Jeff Buzila says the team is still tallying RFP scores, and SaskTel has not responded to either the Horizon (Sept) or Calgary Stampede invites we sent. With no champion identified and no visibility into Zycus's rank in the four-vendor S2P RFP (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba), a deal carried in Best Case risks a silent loss at down-selection and an inflated forecast.

SaskTel is running a four-vendor RFP naming Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba, with SAP Ariba the sitting incumbent the buyer set out to replace, but no scorecard preference has surfaced for any vendor.

The supplier presentation round set for the week of 20 Apr 2026 still has not happened as of 9 Jul 2026 (about 11 weeks overdue), and two Horizon event invites (sent 4-5 May and 4 Jun 2026) went unanswered before a third was sent 8 Jul 2026.

Finalizing the RFP response on 11 Mar 2026 pushed the deal's value up from $225,000 to $357,898 the next day, but no pricing, budget, or economic-buyer conversation has happened with SaskTel to support that number.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This deal survived a competitive four-vendor RFP and reached Shortlisted (25 Nov 2025), but it has been stuck there for roughly 226 days with no economic buyer, no champion, and an 11-week-overdue presentation gate; the close date has already slipped four times and the current one (4 Sep 2026) is not credible without a reschedule.

deal_risk.text: The presentation/interview round scheduled for the week of 20 Apr 2026 is ~11 weeks overdue with no confirmed reschedule, and only 57 days remain to the 4 Sep 2026 close with no visible path through presentation, commercial discussion, and contracting (Next_Step__c).

No economic buyer has been identified or engaged; the deal's only two named contacts (a Procurement Director and an RFP coordinator) have no confirmed authority to approve a $357,898 commitment (Opportunity fields; OpportunityContactRole).

SAP Ariba, the incumbent procurement tool, remains an active RFP competitor, and as of the 8 Jul 2026 Next Step entry it is still unconfirmed whether the CTO or the CIO/EVP who may own that relationship sit on the deciding steering committee (Next_Step__c).

win_position.text: Zycus made the shortlist of a formal four-vendor RFP (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) submitted 11 Mar 2026, positioning it to displace the SAP Ariba incumbent (Opportunity.Competitors__c; Next_Step__c).

No economic buyer or C-level SaskTel contact has ever engaged; Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false and the deal runs through only a Procurement Director and an RFP coordinator (Opportunity fields; OpportunityContactRole).
The supplier-presentation round planned for the week of 20 Apr 2026 has not occurred as of 9 Jul 2026 (~11 weeks overdue), with no champion pushing it forward (Next_Step__c; Next_Step_History__c).

The RFP response finalized 11 Mar 2026 raised deal value from $225,000 to $357,898 (logged 12 Mar 2026), a real commercial commitment from Zycus that has not been matched by any buyer-side pricing or budget discussion (OpportunityFieldHistory).

deal_momentum.text: The only verified two-way buyer contact remains a 13 Apr 2026 email from Jeffrey Buzila (87 days ago) explaining the evaluation was running long due to fiscal year-end reporting; no buyer reply has followed since (Task/Email).
Two Horizon event invites (sent ~4-5 May 2026 and 4 Jun 2026) went unanswered before a third was sent 8 Jul 2026, all rep-initiated with no buyer response (Next_Step__c).
The account transitioned from the prior Zycus rep to Mike Flowers on 13 Apr 2026 and the buyer acknowledged the new point of contact without disruption (Task; OpportunityFieldHistory).
The close date was pushed four months, from 30 Apr 2026 to 4 Sep 2026, on 4 May 2026, with no new milestone confirmed since (OpportunityFieldHistory).

customer_commitment.text: SaskTel ran Zycus through a complete formal RFP cycle: issue (11 Feb 2026), Q&A, submission (11 Mar 2026), and shortlisting (25 Nov 2025), reflecting genuine process-level investment (Next_Step__c; OpportunityFieldHistory).
Outside the scripted RFP milestones, SaskTel has not initiated a single proactive outreach, meeting request, or question since the 13 Apr 2026 email (Task history).

hard.ais_why:
• This is a text-based RFP with no recorded calls, so all evidence comes from the Salesforce record. The strongest written signal is that Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for SaskTel, paired with a Tier 2 "Info Assist (AI Insights)" rating for what they want from AI in the RFP.
• Read together, those two fields say SaskTel's procurement team self-rated AI as a Very Critical evaluation criterion and explicitly wants AI Insights baked into the S2P scope. That self-authored RFP positioning is meaningful buyer-side AI pull, even without a verbatim quote, and it's enough to lift this off the resistant floor.
• What caps the score: the AI tier is Insights, not Agentic or Autonomous; no demo, POC, or champion has been confirmed because there are no Avoma calls; and a text RFP can mask whether the Very Critical rating is genuine excitement or procurement-checklist hygiene. Confidence stays Low until live engagement validates.
